understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each KA59 level by moving every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with `+`, ensuring that when there are multiple goals they are all occupied simultaneously so the engine reports WIN, then progress through all levels until the full run is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each action moves the currently selected piece in one of four directions on a grid where each cell corresponds to 3 pixels; positions are given as pixel coordinates `[x, y]` increasing right/down. Movement can be blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects, and some adjacent objects may be pushable so that attempting to move into them can push them along with the selected piece. Levels can include multiple selectable pieces; control is switched by `CLICK`ing the target piece’s exact on-screen coordinate (same coordinate system as `player.position`/`objects`), after which movement actions apply to that newly selected piece. The `movement.*_blocked` flags are only hints about adjacency and don’t guarantee a move will fail, so actions sometimes need to be tested. Each level has a finite step budget, and running out of steps ends the attempt.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each KA59 level by moving every selectable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with `+`, ensuring that when there are multiple goals they are all occupied simultaneously so the engine reports WIN, then progress through all levels until the full run is won.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each action moves the currently selected piece in one of four directions on a grid where each cell corresponds to 3 pixels; positions are given as pixel coordinates `[x, y]` increasing right/down. Movement can be blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects, and some adjacent objects may be pushable so that attempting to move into them can push them along with the selected piece. Levels can include multiple selectable pieces; control is switched by `CLICK`ing the target piece’s exact on-screen coordinate (same coordinate system as `player.position`/`objects`), after which movement actions apply to that newly selected piece. The `movement.*_blocked` flags are only hints about adjacency and don’t guarantee a move will fail, so actions sometimes need to be tested. Each level has a finite step budget, and running out of steps ends the attempt.
